title: Cluster and Child Processes Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of overcoming Node's single-threaded nature, using the Cluster module for CPU-core balancing, and spawning child processes.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily design philosophy of Node.js that makes the Cluster module necessary for high scale?
options: A system focused on being 'Single-Threaded' by default, meaning it only uses one CPU core at a time.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which Node.js module allows you to create multiple 'worker' processes of your app that all share the same server port?
Which Child Process method is commonly used to 'spawn' a completely new Node.js instance from your main application?
Which and/or what was the primarily benefit of using 'Worker Threads' inside a Node.js project?
options: Executing heavy calculations (like image processing) in parallel without blocking the main event loop.

questions.question: Does following the 'Cluster' model help build truly Fullstack applications by allowing you to utilize every CPU core on a production server?
options: Yes, as it is a core feature of any high-throughput, professional production environment.
